37.reason: [pop function: after printing "error", it still executes pop. Should return to avoid undefined behavior on empty stack.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Missing else statement. When stack is empty, it prints "error " and then also attempts to pop and print an invalid element, causing undefined behavior and incorrect output.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Missing return statement after printing "error " when stack is empty. It will still attempt to pop and print an invalid value.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [pop function: after printing "error", it still executes pop. Should return to avoid undefined behavior on empty stack.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Should not attempt to pop when stack empty; after printing error, it still pops leading to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line executes regardless of whether the stack is empty. If top is -1, it accesses Stack[-1], which is out of bounds. It should be inside an else block or guarded by the error check.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line executes regardless of whether the stack is empty. If top is -1, it accesses Stack[-1], which is out of bounds. It should be inside an else block or guarded by the error check.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Always executes even when stack is empty, leading to undefined behavior (accessing Stack[-1]) after printing "error "]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Always executes even when stack is empty, leading to undefined behavior and incorrect output]

46.reason: [push function: after printing "error" for full stack, it still pushes. Should return to avoid overflow.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Missing else statement. When stack is full, it prints "error " but still pushes the element, causing stack overflow and incorrect behavior.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Missing return statement after printing "error " when stack is full. It will still push the value, causing overflow.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [push function: after printing "error" for full stack, it still pushes. Should return to avoid overflow.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: Should not push when stack full; after printing error, it still pushes leading to overflow.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line executes regardless of whether the stack is full. If top is 99, it increments to 100 and accesses Stack[100], which is out of bounds (array size is 100). It should be inside an else block or guarded by the error check.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This line executes regardless of whether the stack is full. If top is 99, it increments to 100 and accesses Stack[100], which is out of bounds for an array of size 100. It should be inside an else block or guarded by the error check.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Executes even when stack is full, leading to buffer overflow (writing to Stack[100] when top==99)]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Executes even when stack is full, leading to buffer overflow and undefined behavior]
